title: Question: How to set/access "inner" registers from main.cpp
body:
---


Author Name: **Stefan Ludwig**
Original Redmine Message: 1142 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi,

I'm sure this is covered somewhere, but I couldn't find any documentation on this. Sorry for the noise, if this is trivial and I'm just blind...

Here's the problem: I'm trying to set some values in registers deep down in my hierarchy, from within my main.cpp simulation driver. I thought it would be possible to do the following:
```
...
top->a_inst->b_inst->reg[5] = 17;

or

top->v->a_inst->b_inst->reg[5] = 17;
...
top->eval();

```

but when I verilate & c-compile that, there is no v->a_inst field.

What does work, is the following

```
top->v__DOT__a_inst__DOT__b_inst__DOT__reg[5] = 17;

```

I thought I read something somewhere about how to access these things, but I can't find the reference anymore.

How is this done (without DPI!), if it's possible at all?

Thanks!

Cheers,

Stefan
